tagline: An open-weight language stack for India, built in India.
weeklyArc: Sarvam shipped Sarvam-9B-IL this week — an Apache-2.0 model that beats GPT-class systems on nine Indian languages by 22 points on IndicGenBench, runs on a consumer 4090, and was trained with no Western capital. It is the first time an Indian lab has set a credible frontier number on its own benchmark, on its own infrastructure, on its own license.

The team — ex-Google Research and IIT-Madras founders — has held its valuation discipline through the AI hype cycle, raising at price points that look modest next to US labs. The bet has been that an Indian-language frontier model is a category that the global labs will not prioritise quickly enough; the 22-point gap is the proof.

The release matters less for the benchmark than for what it unlocks downstream: every domestic SaaS team that ships in Hindi, Tamil, Marathi, or Bengali now has a self-hostable, license-clean alternative to commercial APIs. A year from now, the visible second-order companies — vernacular voice, regional commerce, tier-2 productivity — will all cite this week as the unblock.
pullQuote: An Indian-language frontier model on Apache-2.0 is the unblock the rest of the country has been waiting for. The visible second-order companies will all cite this week.
